zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir George Barne II於1485年出生在英國時，他的父親George Barnes20歲，母親Elenor Wolrose26歲。他於1524年與Alice Brookes在英國倫敦結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他於1552年被列為lord mayor of london。他去世於1558年2月18日享年73歲，埋葬在英國倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir George Barne II于1485年出生在英国时，他的父亲George Barnes20岁，母亲Elenor Wolrose26岁。他于1524年与Alice Brookes在英国倫敦结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他于1552年被列为lord mayor of london。他去世于1558年2月18日享年73岁，埋葬在英国倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Barne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: relationship name from the Middle English personal name Bern, Barn. In the Midlands and East Anglia this is based on the Scandinavian personal name Biǫrn, anglicized as Beorn; in the south-west it is from Old English Beorn; both names are from a word meaning ‘warrior’. Some of the following surname bearers may alternatively belong with other senses below.

2 English: status name from Middle English barn (Old Scandinavian barn) ‘child’. This term is found in Domesday Book as a byname for men of the upper classes; it might also have had the meaning ‘young man of a prominent family’, like Middle English child ( see Child ).

3 English: locative name, nickname for residence near or employment at a barn, Middle English bern (Old English bere-ærn). See also Barnes . Some of the post-medieval bearers may alternatively belong with (1) or (2).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Barn
meanings: Scottish:  see  Barne   and  compare  Berne  .

variants.name: Barner
meanings: English: in southern England a habitational name for someone who lived by a barn, from Middle English berner ‘dweller by the barn’. Elsewhere possibly a variant of Berner .
Scottish: unexplained.
North German: derivative of the ancient Germanic personal name Barnher or Bernher (see Berner 6).

variants.name: Barnes
meanings: English: habitational name from Barnes (on the Surrey bank of the Thames in London), named with Old English bere-ærn ‘barn, a storehouse for barley and other grain’, or a topographic name or metonymic occupational name for someone who lived by or worked at a barn or barns, from Middle English barn ‘barn, granary’.

English: variant of Barne, with excrescent -s, derived from either the Middle English personal name Bern, Barn (based on the Scandinavian personal name Biǫrn or Old English Beorn, both from a word meaning ‘warrior’), or from Middle English barn (Old Norse barn) ‘child’. The latter term is found as a byname for men of the upper classes; it might also have had the meaning ‘young man of a prominent family’, like Middle English child (see Child ).
Irish: in Ireland in many cases this is no doubt the English name, but in others it is possibly an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Bearáin ‘descendant of Bearán’, a byname meaning ‘spear’.

variants.name: Domesday Book
meanings: English: locative name from either of two places in Lancs, namely Bispham near Blackpool ( Bischopham, 1086 in Domesday Book ) or Bispham in Croston.

variants.name: Berne
meanings: French: from a short form of the personal name Bernard or any other name containing the ancient Germanic element ber(n) ‘bear’.
Altered form of Swiss German   Berni  .
English and Scottish: variant of Barne (see Barnes 2) or Burn .

variants.name: Bearne
meanings: Scots: Bearn, a child, a human creature

variants.name: Byrne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Aidan, Liam, Niall, Declan, Eamonn, Fergus, John Patrick, Keelin, Kieran, Siobhan.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Broin ‘descendant of Bran’, a personal name based on bran ‘raven’. Bran was the name of a son of the King of Leinster, who died at Cologne in 1052, and also of the hero of an 8th century voyage tale.
Irish: in some cases, possibly from Ó Birn ‘descendant of Biorn’, Irish form of the Old Norse personal name Bjǫrn.

variants.name: Child
meanings: English:

nickname from Middle English child ‘child, infant’ (Old English cild), in various possible applications. The word is found in Old English as a byname, and in Middle English as a widely used affectionate term of address. It was also used as a term of status for a young man of noble birth, although the exact meaning is not clear; in the 13th and 14th centuries it was a technical term used of a young noble awaiting elevation to the knighthood. In other cases it may have been applied as a byname to a youth considerably younger than his brothers or to one who was a minor on the death of his father.
in Kent, possibly a topographic name from Old English cielde ‘spring (water)’, a rare word derived from c(e)ald ‘cold’.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir George Barne II was born in 1485, in England, his father, George Barnes, was 20 and his mother, Elenor Wolrose, was 26. He married Alice Brookes in 1524, in London, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 5 daughters. In 1552, his occupation is listed as lord mayor of london. He died on 18 February 1558, at the age of 73, and was buried in London, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir George Barne II est né en 1485, à Angleterre, son père, George Barnes, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Elenor Wolrose, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Alice Brookes en 1524, à Londres, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 5 filles. En 1552, sa profession de son indiquée est lord mayor of london. Il est décédé le 18 février 1558, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterré à Londres, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir George Barne II 1485 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater George Barnes 20 und seine Mutter Elenor Wolrose 26. Er hat 1524 in London, England Alice Brookes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 5 Töchter. 1552 ist sein Beruf als lord mayor of london aufgeführt. Er starb am 18. Februar 1558 im Alter von 73 Jahren und wurde in London, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir George Barne II è nato il nel 1485 nel England, il suo padre, George Barnes, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Elenor Wolrose, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Alice Brookes nel 1524, nel London, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 5 figlie. Nel 1552, la sua occupazione è indicata come lord mayor of london. Morì il 18 febbraio 1558, all'età di 73 anni, e fu sepolto a London, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir George Barne IIが1485年にイギリスで生まれた時，父George Barnesは20歳，母Elenor Wolroseは26歳であった。1524年にイギリスロンドンでAlice Brookesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。1552年，職業はlord mayor of londonと記録されている。彼は1558年2月18日に73歳で死去し，イギリスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir George Barne II(이)가 1485년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 George Barnes(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Elenor Wolrose(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1524년 영국 런던에서 Alice Brookes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1552년, 그의 직업은 lord mayor of london이었다. 그는 1558년 2월 18일 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir George Barne II nasceu em 1485, em Inglaterra, seu pai, George Barnes, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Elenor Wolrose, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice Brookes em 1524, em London, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 5 filhas. Em 1552, sua profissão aparece como lord mayor of london. Ele faleceu em 18 de fevereiro de 1558, com 73 anos, e foi sepultado em London, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir George Barne II родился в 1485 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени George Barnes было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Elenor Wolrose, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Brookes в 1524 г. в местности Лондон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 5 дочерей. В 1552 году он работал по профессии lord mayor of london. Он умер 18 февраля 1558 г. в возрасте 73 лет и был похоронен в местности Лондон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir George Barne II nació en 1485, en Inglaterra, su papá, George Barnes, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Elenor Wolrose, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Alice Brookes en 1524, en London, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 5 hijas. En 1552, su ocupación registrada era lord mayor of london. Murió el 18 de febrero de 1558, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultado en London, Inglaterra.
